regions.us-ports: Today Miami has a Heat Advisory in effect with current conditions ESE 7kt, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability; San Diego and Los Angeles also have Heat Advisories with heat index values near 87F and 102F respectively. Today’s ESE 7kt wind, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability in Miami support routine embarkation and disembarkation operations, while shore excursions and on-deck activities should incorporate the Heat Advisory into staffing and hydration plans. Over the next 24 to 48 hours Miami is forecast ESE 15kt with rain and less than 10% rain probability on Day 1 and SE 14kt with rain and less than 10% rain probability on Day 2, and Port Everglades and Port Canaveral should prepare for ESE to SE 14–15kt winds that can affect docking maneuvers and tender transfers. Beyond 48 hours Miami is forecast ESE 12kt with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability on Day 3, which will influence gangway visibility and could require minor scheduling adjustments for open-deck events.
